mechanism_narrative: Persistent near-zero short-term rates force investors to reach for yield, compressing term premiums and raising long-term yields as the economy strengthens, causing the 10-year yield to rise over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 10-year yield falls in more than 60% of 20-day windows when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% for the prior month.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated unemployment at 7.8% masks an improving labor market; once UNRATE falls below 7%, the market prices in tighter labor conditions and rising wage inflation, lifting the 10-year yield over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If, after UNRATE crosses below 7.0%, the 10-year yield declines in at least three consecutive instances within the subsequent 20-day window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 900k combined with a federal funds rate below 0.5% signals robust residential investment with no imminent monetary tightening, boosting growth expectations and pushing the 10-year yield upwards over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In instances where HOUST > 900 and FEDFUNDS < 0.5 simultaneously, the 10-year yield must rise in at least 60% of the following 20-day windows; otherwise reject.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production rising more than 1% month-over-month indicates expanding supply constraints, which historically precede higher core inflation and cause the 10-year yield to rise over the subsequent 20 trading days during industrial upswings.
failure_condition: If, when INDPRO increases more than 1% month-over-month, the 10-year yield falls in the subsequent 20 days a majority of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) reflects strong consumer spending, a leading indicator of GDP growth; this demand strength lifts near-term growth forecasts and raises the 10-year yield within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If positive month-over-month growth in RSAFS is accompanied by a falling 10-year yield in more than half of the subsequent 5-trading-day windows over a 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI increases above 0.2% signal inflationary pressure that the market expects will prompt eventual Fed tightening; this anticipation drives the 10-year yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If, following a CPIAUCSL increase of more than 0.2%, the 10-year yield does not rise in at least 70% of subsequent 20-trading-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in payrolls (PAYEMS > +200k) and retail sales (RSAFS increasing) confirms robust domestic demand and a tightening labor market, a combination that historically pushes the 10-year yield up over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases >200,000 and RSAFS increases month-over-month
failure_condition: If, when both conditions hold, the 10-year yield is lower 20 days later in more than 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A two-month decline in industrial production signals the onset of a manufacturing recession, prompting a flight to safety that lowers the 10-year yield over the next 60 trading days, particularly in a post-expansion regime.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: If, after two consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO, the 10-year yield rises in more than 50% of the following 60-trading-day windows.
